issues: FACTUAL ERROR: Article states NFC was 'als NFC Forum-Standard veröffentlicht' in '2003'. Web search confirms: The NFC Forum was founded on March 18, 2004 by Nokia, Philips (NXP) and Sony — NOT 2003. The founding announcement (nfc-forum.org, globenewswire.com, phys.org) confirms 2004. Score -15.
Philips (heute NXP Semiconductors) and Sony as co-developers of NFC: CONFIRMED — both companies were founding members of NFC Forum in 2004.
ISO/IEC 14443 as technical base for NFC contactless cards: CONFIRMED — standard technical knowledge.
13,56 MHz operating frequency for NFC: CONFIRMED.
eGK G2 Dual Interface (ISO 7816 + ISO 14443): confirmed standard technical knowledge.

articles.fixInstructions: Fix NFC Forum founding year: Replace '2003 als NFC Forum-Standard veröffentlicht' with '2004 als NFC Forum-Standard etabliert' (NFC Forum gegründet am 18. März 2004 von Nokia, Philips/NXP und Sony). The underlying technology (NFCIP-1, ISO/IEC 18092) was developed from 2002 onward, but the NFC Forum as a standardization body was formally established in 2004.

issues: DataMatrix 'als internationaler Standard ISO/IEC 16022 veröffentlicht' in 2000: The article states '2000 als internationaler Standard ISO/IEC 16022 veröffentlicht'. Web search and ISO.org (confirmed via Playwright) confirm: ISO/IEC 16022 first edition was published in 2000, second edition in 2006, third (current) in 2024. The claim of '2000' for the original publication is technically accurate.

Source URL links to ISO/IEC 16022:2006: The source URL (iso.org/standard/44230.html) points to the 2006 edition. ISO page confirmed LIVE via Playwright — title: 'ISO/IEC 16022:2006 — Information technology — Automatic identification and data capture'. The 2006 edition is now 'Withdrawn' (superseded by 2024 edition). Article should ideally reference ISO/IEC 16022:2024. Minor staleness. Score -5.
